codeReview.summary: The feature adds a SQLite-backed performance tracker, reporting helpers, bankroll integration, and CSV export, but the CLV implementation is not reliable enough to satisfy the feature contract. Closing-line selection is not tied to the sportsbook that supplied the opening bet, and the automatic settlement path only syncs CLV against whatever snapshot already exists at settlement time, so later closing updates are missed unless a caller manually reruns the sync helper.

issues.description: sync_closing_lines_from_snapshots() selects `ORDER BY fetched_at DESC, id DESC LIMIT 1` from `odds_snapshots` using only `(game_pk, market_type)`. The new `bet_performance` table in `src/db.py` stores no `book_name`, even though odds ingestion and edge calculation are bookmaker-specific (`src/clients/odds_client.py` and `src/engine/edge_calculator.py`). In any multi-book game this lets CLV be computed from an arbitrary sportsbook's closing line instead of the book that produced `odds_at_bet`, which makes the per-bet CLV mathematically wrong.

record_bet_settlement() only calls sync_closing_lines_from_snapshots() once, during settlement, with whatever odds rows already exist. If the actual closing snapshot is persisted later, the tracker never refreshes automatically; `src/pipeline/daily.py` finalizes picks immediately after placement/freeze without a later close-line fetch, and the new tests have to seed a later snapshot then call sync_closing_lines_from_snapshots() manually to get non-zero CLV. A local repro against a temporary SQLite DB showed `after_settle (-130, 0.0)`, `after_late_snapshot_no_sync (-130, 0.0)`, and only `after_manual_sync (-140, 0.018115942028985588)`, so normal runs do not reliably store the required close-vs-open CLV.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared state does not document how a post-placement closing snapshot is supposed to be captured for CLV. `.factory/services.yaml` only exposes the once-per-run daily pipeline commands, and `.factory/library/` has no CLV/performance-tracker note, even though correct CLV depends on a second odds snapshot after the bet is placed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml defines `pipeline.daily` / `pipeline.daily_dryrun` only; no manifest entry or library note describes a close-line capture step, while `src/ops/performance_tracker.py` only updates CLV when `sync_closing_lines_from_snapshots()` is rerun after a newer snapshot exists.

summary: Review failed. Reporting/export support landed, but CLV is still operationally incorrect: it can use the wrong sportsbook's closing line and it does not automatically refresh when a later closing snapshot arrives.
